title: Canadian water-policy papers 1–2: agricultural water pollution and farmland consolidation risk (2026)
description: Two independent research papers on agricultural water pollution in Canada. Paper 1 (Who Actually Pays for Water Pollution in Canada?) estimates fiscal incidence of water-pollution management costs. Paper 2 (The Consolidation of Risk) examines how farmland consolidation raises the probability that large title holders become enforcement or recovery targets. Author: John Crandlemire, P.Eng. Independent analysis; Newcopia Intelligence disclosed in Acknowledgments only. Paper 3 of the series is reserved for a later release.

creators.name: Crandlemire, John
creators.affiliation: Independent research, High River, Alberta, Canada
